text.title: Conversational Multi-Agent Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for shipping an AutoGen-style conversational runner that terminates safely and stays within budget.

rule.statement: Every conversational runner MUST set BOTH a non-empty `termination_phrase` (or JSON-flag output check) AND a `max_turns` cap. Neither alone is sufficient.
rule.rationale: Phrase alone risks infinite loops (model never says it); max_turns alone yields no semantic completion signal — the run terminates mid-thought.

rule.statement: `max_turns` MUST be ≤ 30. Higher values almost always indicate the wrong pattern (conversational masquerading as a long pipeline).
rule.rationale: Runs that legitimately need >30 turns are not conversational — they are open-ended chats or pipelines mis-labelled.

rule.statement: Per-turn prompt MUST include only the last N messages from the conversation (default N=3, max N=8). Full history passing is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Full-history passing makes turn cost grow O(N²); fixed window keeps per-turn cost O(1) at the price of forgetting old turns (a property, not a bug).

rule.statement: The runner MUST track cumulative `used_tokens` after every turn and abort the loop when `used_tokens ≥ 0.9 × token_budget`, emitting the current trace with `aborted_early=true`.
rule.rationale: Conversational + sliding window still accumulates output tokens with turn count; cumulative budget audit is the only reliable hard stop besides max_turns.

rule.statement: Agents in the conversation MUST have distinct `name` AND non-identical `system_prompt`. Two agents with the same prompt degenerate to monologue.
rule.rationale: Distinct names without distinct prompts produce indistinguishable turns; the conversation does not advance.
